title: The Busy Day of Disco and Disco Moles
summary: In this subterranean adventure, Papa Mole and his adventurous children, Disco and Disco, embark on an action-packed journey through their favorite spots. From a magical toy store featuring a toy toilet to a zoo bustling with baby animals, the mole family explores the world with curiosity and joy. Their day reaches a peak at the mole playground, where they navigate tunnels and conquer the heights of Mole Mountain. To conclude their day, the moles engage in 'tunnel mischief,' digging elaborate paths for friends, including a cavernous tunnel perfect for their bear friend, Zingo, to begin his winter hibernation.

highlightQuote.text: Actually, there's no hula hoops in here, but there's a toy toilet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that one lights up all red.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's tell a story. So I got a storybook that gives us ideas for stories that we want to tell.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe we should tell a story about these. These are Disco, Disco, could do any of these ideas. And these ideas or these ideas, or these ideas, or these ideas, or these ideas, or these ideas.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How about they could do these ideas and these ideas. Those are pet ideas and these are animal ideas. And they could do these ideas and these ideas. Those are the ideas that they're going to do. So let's tell our story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wait, I didn't get the idea.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Actually, I think you did get it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was just ideas from the book. The book got it. These are the ideas that we were going to pick.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, can you read them to me?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They're... we picked this idea.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How about this idea? This is the car one, this is the baby's one, this is the play one, and this is the puppy one, and this is the animal one. Now you really got those four.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wait, so tell me again there's... the baby and the puppy and the animals...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And the toys. And the toys. So Papa Moles says, 'Kid Moles, we got a lot of stuff planned for today.' And Disco and Disco say, 'We do! What comes first?' First thing is... the playball store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'The playball store,' says Disco. 'What's that?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's a store with all kinds of toys. 'Oh, let's go, let's go, let's go!' Zoom, zoom, zoom, zoom, zoom! Park. We're at the store. So this is the toy store. This is our toy store. And you can pick anything you want and you can play with it inside the store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, thank you. Do we get to bring anything home?' says Disco.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. It's not okay to bring anything home.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco says, 'Ooh, is that a hula hoop? I want to try it.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where's the hula hoop? Oh, it's over here. Oh, here it is. Oh, it's a nice sparkly green hula hoop. Actually, there's no hula hoops in here, but there's a toy toilet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco says, 'Oh, a toy toilet. What do I do with it?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We pee into it. We just pretend pee though, right? Uh-huh. And there's a flusher.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'What else have they got here?' says Disco.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There's toy chairs and another toy toilet. Oh, there's a slide up here and even a room with one teacher.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I think we got some other stuff planned.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. 'Let's do the next thing,' says Disco and Disco.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This is the next thing. Play cars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, time to play cars. Okay. Where do we do that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Play, play, play, play, play, play, play, play. Now it's this idea to take a bath. Splash! Now it's this idea, Moles.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what's next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: This idea. This idea.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's so dark I can't see it. Let me bring it over to the light here. Oh, I see. A picture of bedtime, and a lamp, and a baby, and some slippers.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Play, get bed. Play, get bed. Play, get bed. Play, get and bed.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we're going to pretend to get in bed?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wake up!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We're awake.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Play cars, play cars some more. Did that, then ate breakfast. Did that, play pet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, pet our pets. Pet, pet, pet.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And that's the last thing. Go to the zoo!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh my goodness, that's my favorite,' says Disco. And Disco says, 'Yeah, I like the zoo.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Zoom, zoom, zoom, zoom, zoom! Park. 'Oh, look at that!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what do you see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: 'Look at that and that! Look at those baby animals!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, I love to see the baby animals,' says Disco. Now where's the giraffe? Do you think we could find a giraffe?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There's no giraffe. But there's a different pet zoo. There's this, there's these we can see. We can see everything in this of the babies. I don't think we are going to do that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Play outside.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, playing outside. That's our last thing. Awesome. What do we do when we're playing outside? And Disco says, 'Chase!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The playground.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, let's go to the playground. Zoom, zoom, zoom, zoom. 'Oh, we parked at your favorite mole playground!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, it has so many tunnels to zoom around in. And then we'll dig some more.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And some other things too as well. Some more some other things for more that moles like. Creepy crawly bouncers, nice bug hills, sluggy things, and some more toys, and an obstacle course and cars. This is the best mole playground ever!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It makes sense.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. Swing, swing, swing, swing, swing, swing, swing, swing. 'I love swinging,' says Disco. And Disco says, 'I'm going to try the mole mountain.' Climb, climb, climb, climb. 'I'm on top of the mountain!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then you slide down, down, down.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now the moles can do whatever they want.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco and Disco say, 'Wow, we did so many things today. We had a very big day.' 'Oh, but we didn't do any mischief!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my goodness, you're right.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's do mischief.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, we're going to do one last mischievous thing. But where should we go to do it? Some place we've never done mischief before.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh that... Daddy, this basket's making stars from the side.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, the light from the other basket makes stars on that one. That's really cool.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do you still have that flashlight? Or did you put it away?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I didn't have a flashlight... oh, the red one. There it is.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco and Disco said, 'Let's do mischief with flashlights. Ha ha ha!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Actually, flashlights aren't for mischief. Tunnel mischief.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, let's do tunnel mischief, says Disco. Okay. How do we do tunnel mischief? Do we just dig a lot of tunnels?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We make different kinds of tunnels and silly ones.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a silly tunnel.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Silly tunnels. I'm going to make a tiny, tiny tunnel that's the size of... it's so small only a tiny worm can go in it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Speaking of that, I think I want to... I think we can tell the rest of the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Well, Disco and Disco made silly tunnels all the rest of that day. They made crazy spiral tunnels and up-and-down tunnels and deep tunnels and a tunnel that went down under the ground and then came back up again in a different place.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And what's other silly tunnels that they did?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I don't know, we have to think of them.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There was... star tunnel and lamp tunnel and cup tunnel and mouse tunnel and silly tunnel and secret tunnels and really damp tunnels and animal tunnels.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Animal tunnels, yeah. Okay, for our last mischief tunnel, says Disco, we're going to build the biggest tunnel ever and then a big bear can go in there to sleep. And so they dug a very big tunnel, it was much bigger than a mole. In fact, it was so big it was just the right size for a bear to hibernate.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then their friend the bear... what was their bear friend's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Zingo.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Zingo came and said, 'Excuse me, that looks like a very comfortable cave.' And they said, 'It's not a cave, it's a bear tunnel.' 'Oh, well, either way I'd love to sleep in it. May I?' And they said, 'Yes.' And so Zingo crawled right in and curled up and then made the bear sleep sound, which is like this...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or... [Snoring sounds]

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's called snoring.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's true, that is called snoring.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then Disco and Disco were very tired from their long day, and so they decided to go to sleep.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I think we are first whispering are over, but I want to do some shadow puppets.

characters.name: Papa Mole
characters.description: The wise leader and industrious architect of the Mischief Moles, this polite elder visionary guides his kin from the attic’s shadowed heights toward the stars and across biting, crystalline ice. A master pathfinder who initiated their voyage upon unearthing an ancient tome, he balances the weight of celestial navigation with the tactile craft of carving a new tunnel home and the thoughtful devotion of planning whimsical days of activities for his children. Whether introducing surprise robots for play, anchoring meditations during a frozen trek, or sleep-sitting in his favorite chair, his tactical brilliance ensures their furthest discoveries remain rooted in the rhythmic, quiet peace of a sanctuary built by his own seasoned hands.

characters.name: Kid Moles
characters.description: The group name for Papa Mole's children, Disco and Disco.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Zingo
characters.description: A bear friend of the moles who hibernates in their largest tunnel.

characters.name: Teacher
characters.description: A figure found lingering in the dim recesses of the toy store, acting as the quiet custodian who provides the group with the loan of a heavy, brass school bell.

characters.name: Tiny worm
characters.description: A very small creature for whom the moles dig a tiny, custom tunnel.

characters.name: Baby animals
characters.description: The young creatures the moles visit during their trip to the zoo.

places.name: Playball store
places.description: A sprawling toy store where children are encouraged to play with anything they find.

places.name: Zoo
places.description: A park where the moles visit and admire various baby animals.

places.name: Mole playground
places.description: An outdoor area filled with tunnels, obstacle courses, and bugs.

places.name: Mole Mountain
places.description: A tall climbing peak at the playground that features a long slide.

places.name: Bear tunnel
places.description: The largest and most comfortable tunnel built by the moles for Zingo the bear's hibernation.
